text:
Market today: Mixed but leaning cautious. Core inflation hit a multi-month high, which keeps the Fed on hold longer — that's a headwind for high-multiple tech and chip names. Micron's blowout premarket jump is a tailwind for semis specifically, which helps AVGO and SMH today.

Your holdings: Three need attention. AVGO (-12.45%) is your largest position and already flagged TRIM — on margin, this hole is real; watch whether it gets a sympathy lift from Micron or fades. PLTR (-12.77%) is flagged SELL — it's down hard and your stop at $85 is well below current price, but the trend is not your friend; tighten your thinking here. SPAL (-14.48%) is a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily — that loss compounds faster than a normal position and margin makes it worse. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile, and thinly traded — handle manually, small size.

Watch: 1) How AVGO trades today — Micron halo could give you a trim opportunity at better prices. 2) Whether inflation headlines start dragging QQQ below recent support.

Bottom line: Use any Micron-driven pop in semis to trim AVGO and cut what needs cutting before the inflation story reasserts itself.
